parts.answer: source of error improvement yeast not completely mixed in solutions; stir for longer or shake / stir until dissolved AW; time yeast was in each solution / start time not the same; record start time for each solution / measure time for each solution separately; top of mixture not level so difficult to measure height / measurement of height may be incorrect or inaccurate; average of high and low point measurement for each time / use graduated test-tube or measuring cylinder; test-tubes may be different sizes; use test-tubes which are similar / identical / same diameter; some yeast stuck to bag / rod / spilled; provide yeast in suitable containers AW; temperature of water difficult to control / adjust AW; use thermostatically–controlled water–bath; AVP e.g. starting line too thick; AVP use thinner marker pen; enzymes deactivated / denatured; no growth / respiration / carbon dioxide / bubbles / increase in height / volume AW in boiled yeast ORA;
